incident_text: An employee opened an attachment, after which local and network files were encrypted and normal access stopped. The event was escalated because the affected system processed sensitive information.
A profile field stored active client-side markup that triggered when the page loaded. Relevant authentication, endpoint, and access logs were retained for investigation.
A malicious process encrypted shared files and deleted accessible backup copies before displaying recovery instructions.
The public service received an extreme surge of requests from distributed sources and became unavailable to legitimate users. The source had not previously been associated with normal activity.
The public application received crafted requests that exploited an input-validation weakness and exposed protected application data.
The account was locked after a high-rate sequence of invalid credentials from an unfamiliar address.
A downloaded document attempted to run an embedded script and install a background process. Analysts requested containment and a review of related accounts and systems.
An internal user altered protected records despite having no assigned responsibility for those accounts.
A user account was accessed from a new region, followed by changes to the recovery email and multi-factor authentication settings. The source had not previously been associated with normal activity.
The web service was compromised through an unpatched application component exposed to the internet. The activity differed significantly from the recent operational baseline.
Inbound traffic exceeded normal capacity and exhausted the web gateway's connection resources.
A profile field stored active client-side markup that triggered when the page loaded. The source had not previously been associated with normal activity.
An urgent message pretending to be from management asked staff to open an unexpected document and confirm their identity.
A downloaded document attempted to run an embedded script and install a background process. Analysts requested containment and a review of related accounts and systems.
An infected endpoint generated periodic encrypted connections consistent with command-and-control beaconing. Analysts requested containment and a review of related accounts and systems.
A login request contained database operators designed to alter the intended query and bypass authentication. The event was escalated because the affected system processed sensitive information.
A distributed packet flood saturated network bandwidth and caused intermittent service outages. The activity differed significantly from the recent operational baseline.
A database administrator exported sensitive information without an approved business purpose. The event was escalated because the affected system processed sensitive information.

The account was locked after a high-rate sequence of invalid credentials from an unfamiliar address.

An administrator account was used from an unknown device to create another privileged user.
An internet-connected device repeatedly communicated with known control infrastructure without user activity.
A comment field contained executable browser code intended to run when another user viewed the page. Relevant authentication, endpoint, and access logs were retained for investigation.
